what_manipulated: presence of facial infection cues on in-group members
what_measured: accuracy and type of recall errors in attribution task
experiment: Participants viewed a slideshow of eight individuals (statements paired with photos), then in a surprise recall task attempted to match statements to faces. In control, all faces healthy; in treatment, in-group faces bore infection cues (facial rash). Error patterns tested whether healthy out-group faces were confounded with infected in-group faces in memory.

what_manipulated: presence of gender in professional scenario and judgment framing (egalitarian vs. Bayesian)
what_measured: evaluation of third party's judgment and own probability judgment of professional role
experiment: Across studies, participants evaluated a third party's Bayesian judgment comparing gender-matched individuals in professions (e.g., man vs. woman who performed surgery), rating fairness/accuracy/intelligence. Study 4-5 participants made own probability judgments of role assignment (doctor, pilot) given identical behavior across genders, then evaluated third party making Bayesian judgment.

what_manipulated: age group (4-year-olds, 6-year-olds, adults) and task difficulty via task procedure version
what_measured: retrieval dependency (contingency of success across associations within multi-element events)
experiment: Participants learned multi-element events (scene, person, object) then performed cued recognition across all element-pair combinations. Dependency was computed as proportion of joint retrieval (both correct/both incorrect) minus predictions of independent model. Experiment 1 examined age effects in dependency; Experiment 2 dampened accuracy in 6-year-olds via same adult procedure to test dissociation between overall accuracy and dependency.

what_manipulated: stimulus type (bouba-like vs. kiki-like pseudowords; rounded vs. spiky shapes; arousal level of pseudowords)
what_measured: arousal ratings (Exp 1); acoustic feature predictions of arousal (Exp 2); probability of shape matching in 2AFC task (Exp 3)
experiment: Experiment 1: Participants rated arousal of shapes and words separately on 5-point scale. Experiment 2: New participants rated arousal of 929 novel pseudowords; acoustic features extracted and regression models built to predict arousal of prior bouba-kiki words. Experiment 3 (preregistered): Participants heard pseudowords varying in arousal, performed 2AFC task matching each to spiky or rounded shape.

what_manipulated: diagnostic group (GAD, healthy control, high trait anxiety) and facial emotion (angry, fearful, happy, neutral)
what_measured: reaction time to peripheral targets; disengagement and engagement components of spatial attention orienting
experiment: Experiment 1: Participants viewed centrally presented faces (three emotions) followed by brief peripheral target letter; reaction time to identify target measured disengagement. Experiment 2: Peripheral cuing task (face cue location varied; target valid/invalid) and central cuing task (eye gaze direction) measured spatial orienting. Diagnostic group status and emotion type crossed within emotional attention task.

what_manipulated: instruction condition: augmented feedback (aF), external focus (EF), and/or monetary reward (RE) in countermovement jumping
what_measured: maximal jump height, ground reaction forces, joint angles, muscular activity (rectus femoris, gastrocnemius, tibialis, biceps femoris)
experiment: Within-subjects: 18 participants performed six maximal countermovement jumps across six instruction conditions (neutral, aF, RE, aF+EF, aF+RE, aF+EF+RE), each repeated twice in randomized order. Kinetic and kinematic data recorded via force plate and motion capture; electromyography measured muscle activity during the jump.
